zh.siteSubtitle: 据传为爱尔兰圣人马拉基所著（1595年出版）
zh.homeIntro1: 《教皇预言》（Prophetia Sancti Malachiae Archiepiscopi, de Summis Pontificibus）是一份包含112条简短拉丁语箴言的文献，据称每条箴言分别描述一位从策肋定二世（1143年即位）起的历任教皇，直至最后一位教皇「罗马人伯多禄」（Petrus Romanus）。该文献署名为12世纪爱尔兰阿马总主教圣马拉基（1094-1148），声称他于1139年访问罗马时获得神启。
zh.homeIntro2: 然而，这份文献直到1595年才由本笃会修士阿诺德·维翁（Arnold Wion）首次出版。绝大多数历史学家认为它是16世纪晚期的伪作——1595年之前的箴言与对应教皇高度吻合（因为可以事后编写），而之后的箴言则含糊得多。最有力的证据是：该文献在1590年教皇选举会议中首次出现，疑似用于支持特定候选人。
zh.homeIntro3: 尽管学术界普遍认为其系伪托之作，《教皇预言》仍然是天主教传统中最广为流传的预言文献之一。特别是关于「最后一位教皇」的预言在每次教皇选举时都会引发广泛关注。本站精选了22条最具代表性的箴言，涵盖早期的精确匹配和近现代的争议性解读。
zh.coreQuote: 「在圣教会遭受最后的迫害时，罗马人伯多禄将登上教皇宝座。他将在诸多磨难中牧养羊群。此后，七丘之城将被摧毁，可畏的审判者将审判万民。」
keyPointsList: 1139年 —— 圣马拉基据称访问罗马并获得神启（无同时代文献记载此事）
1595年 —— 本笃会修士阿诺德·维翁在《生命之树》中首次出版该预言
1820年 —— 第一批系统性学术批评出现，质疑预言的真实性
2005年 —— 本笃十六世当选，被认为对应第111条「橄榄的荣耀」
2013年 —— 方济各当选，争论他是否为预言中的最后一位教皇
sourceList: 预言拉丁原文出自1595年阿诺德·维翁《生命之树》（Lignum Vitae），现代文本参考多个学术版本（Wikipedia: Prophecy of the Popes）
教皇历史信息参考天主教百科全书与梵蒂冈官方记录（Vatican.va）
验证评价基于历史记录和学术研究，编者观点不代表学术定论
站点图标采用教皇三重冕（Papal Tiara）——天主教教皇权威最具辨识度的视觉符号

en.siteSubtitle: Attributed to St. Malachy of Armagh (published 1595)
en.homeTitle: What is the Prophecy of the Popes?
en.homeIntro1: The Prophecy of the Popes (Prophetia Sancti Malachiae Archiepiscopi, de Summis Pontificibus) is a series of 112 short Latin phrases, each purportedly describing a pope from Celestine II (elected 1143) to the final pope, 'Peter the Roman' (Petrus Romanus). The text is attributed to Saint Malachy (1094-1148), Archbishop of Armagh, Ireland, who allegedly received the vision during a visit to Rome in 1139.
en.homeIntro2: However, the document was not published until 1595 by Benedictine monk Arnold Wion. Most historians consider it a late 16th-century forgery — the mottos before 1595 match their corresponding popes with remarkable precision (since they could have been written after the fact), while those after 1595 are far more vague. The strongest evidence: the document first surfaced during the 1590 papal conclave, likely to promote a specific candidate.
en.homeIntro3: Despite widespread scholarly consensus that it is a pseudepigraphical work, the Prophecy of the Popes remains one of the most popular prophetic texts in Catholic tradition. The prophecy of the 'last pope' draws renewed attention with every papal election. This site presents 22 of the most representative mottos, covering both the precise early matches and the controversial modern interpretations.
en.coreQuote: "In the final persecution of the Holy Roman Church, there will sit Peter the Roman, who will pasture his sheep in many tribulations. When these things are finished, the city of seven hills will be destroyed, and the dreadful Judge will judge his people."
keyPointsList: 1139 —— St. Malachy allegedly visits Rome and receives the vision (no contemporary documentation)
1595 —— Benedictine monk Arnold Wion first publishes the prophecy in Lignum Vitae
1820 —— First systematic scholarly critiques questioning the prophecy's authenticity
2005 —— Benedict XVI elected, linked to motto #111 'Gloria Olivae' (Glory of the Olive)
2013 —— Francis elected, debate over whether he is the prophesied last pope
sourceList: Latin text from Arnold Wion's Lignum Vitae (1595), modern text cross-referenced with multiple scholarly editions (Wikipedia: Prophecy of the Popes)
Papal history from the Catholic Encyclopedia and Vatican official records (Vatican.va)
Verification based on historical records and academic research; editorial opinions do not represent scholarly consensus
Site icon: Papal Tiara — the most recognizable visual symbol of papal authority in Catholic tradition
